name: Simplifying Golf Academy
address: 640 Old Hickory Blvd, Old Hickory, TN 37138
about: Simplifying Golf Academy is a professional golf training facility located at Riverside Golf Center in Old Hickory, TN. The academy, led by PGA-certified pro Michel 'Barney' Alary, offers individualized golf instruction and coaching for players of all skill levels, from beginners to advanced golfers. Utilizing a Flightscope Launch Monitor and climate-controlled hitting area, the facility provides year-round training focused on improving swing mechanics, impact consistency, and course management. Lessons are typically 45 to 60 minutes and emphasize practical, results-oriented teaching over aesthetic swing changes. The academy also offers custom club fitting services with Ping golf equipment and has a proven track record of helping students earn college scholarships and improve their game significantly.
why_golfers_like_it: Utilizes advanced Flightscope Launch Monitor technology for precise swing data
Personalized instruction from a PGA professional with 30+ years of experience
Climate-controlled hitting area allows for year-round practice and lessons
Focus on practical, results-oriented swing mechanics rather than aesthetics
Custom club fitting services with Ping golf equipment available on-site

simulator_hardware.notes: Launch monitor used to quantify swing and results

faq.question: Do I need to bring my own clubs?
faq.answer: Yes, you should bring your own golf clubs for lessons. The facility also offers custom club fitting services with Ping golf equipment if you're interested in new clubs.

faq.question: Can I bring my own food and drink?
faq.answer: The website does not specify food and drink policies, so it's recommended to contact them directly regarding outside food and beverages.

faq.question: How long does an 18-hole simulation take?
faq.answer: The website primarily focuses on individual lessons and practice sessions rather than full 18-hole simulations. Lesson times are typically 45-60 minutes, and the facility's setup seems geared toward instruction rather than extended play.

faq.question: Is the facility kid-friendly?
faq.answer: Yes, the academy offers junior coaching programs and has a history of helping young golfers earn college scholarships, indicating a kid-friendly environment suitable for youth golf instruction.
